# Vidacity

> Vidacity is a free, open-source native SwiftUI YouTube client for iPhone and iPad. It plays YouTube videos ad-free, signs in with the user's real YouTube account via cookies (no Google API key), supports background audio, AirPlay, and Picture-in-Picture, and offers offline downloads from YouTube plus ~2,000 other sites supported by yt-dlp. Distributed via source on GitHub for sideload installation, not available on the App Store.

## Key facts

- **What it is:** Native iOS / iPadOS YouTube client.
- **Platforms:** iOS 17.0 and later. Universal (iPhone + iPad). Runs on Apple Silicon Mac via "Designed for iPad".
- **Distribution:** GitHub source only. Sideload via Xcode (free Apple ID works, 7-day expiry; paid Apple Developer Program extends to 1 year). Not on the App Store.
- **License:** MIT-style open source.
- **Source code:** https://github.com/leokossdev/vidacity
- **Website:** https://vidacity.app/
- **Made with:** SwiftUI, AVPlayer, WKWebView (login).

## Core libraries

- **yt-dlp:** stream URL extraction for YouTube and ~2,000 other sites. Auto-updates weekly inside the app.
- **YouTubeKit** (b5i/YouTubeKit): cookie-based YouTube data layer. No Google API key required.
- **FFmpeg:** in-process muxing and transcoding (H.264, MP3) compiled as a static iOS library.
- **YoutubeDL-iOS** (kewlbear/YoutubeDL-iOS): brings yt-dlp + Python to iOS via PythonKit.
- **Kingfisher:** disk-cached image loader for thumbnails and avatars.
- **LNPopupUI:** expanding mini-player above the tab bar (Apple Music / Podcasts interaction model).
- **KeychainAccess:** secure Keychain storage for the user's YouTube session cookies.
- **SwimplyPlayIndicator:** animated audio bars for the currently-playing item.

## Features

- Watch YouTube ad-free with the official mobile-app surface (home, search, channel, playlist, video detail, comments).
- Sign in with a real YouTube account via in-app WKWebView. Cookies stored only in iOS Keychain, never on disk or in logs.
- Subscriptions, watch history, user playlists, liked videos, Watch Later. All synced from YouTube.
- Like / dislike / save-to-playlist actions on videos.
- Read, write, reply to, like, dislike, and translate comments.
- Background audio with lock-screen controls and Now Playing.
- AirPlay and Picture-in-Picture supported.
- Offline downloads of any played video (implicit) and via an explicit Download button.
- Dedicated "Link" tab. Paste any URL from ~2,000 yt-dlp-supported sites (YouTube, Vimeo, X/Twitter, TikTok, Instagram, SoundCloud, Reddit, Twitch, Dailymotion, Bilibili, ...) and pick video + audio format with optional H.264 / MP3 transcoding.
- Three-tier playback fallback handling PoT-locked content (Proof-of-Origin Tokens).
- Localized in English, Spanish, Russian, German, and French.
- Dark mode only, because the whole UI is designed for it.
- No telemetry, no analytics, no remote logging.

## Installation summary

1. Install Xcode (16 or newer) on macOS.
2. Add a free Apple ID in Xcode → Settings → Accounts.
3. `git clone https://github.com/leokossdev/vidacity.git`
4. Open `Vidacity/Vidacity.xcodeproj`.
5. In the Vidacity target → Signing & Capabilities, change the Bundle Identifier to something unique to you, set Team to your Personal Team.
6. Enable Developer Mode on the iPhone or iPad (Settings → Privacy & Security → Developer Mode → On). Device reboots.
7. Connect the device, press ⌘R in Xcode.
8. On first launch: Settings → General → VPN & Device Management → trust the developer profile.

Free-signed builds expire after 7 days. Use AltStore (https://altstore.io) for invisible auto-refresh, or upgrade to a paid Apple Developer Program ($99 / year) for 1-year certificates.

## Why not on the App Store

Apple App Store Review Guideline 5.2.3 prohibits unofficial YouTube clients (apps that significantly replicate the YouTube experience). A public App Store release is therefore not possible. Sideload / TestFlight is the only legitimate distribution path.

## Privacy & security

- All YouTube authentication is cookie-based via an ephemeral `.nonPersistent()` `WKWebsiteDataStore` (no shared session state).
- Cookies are persisted only in the iOS Keychain, never written to disk, UserDefaults, plist, or logs.
- Signed stream URLs are kept in-memory only with a 30-minute TTL.
- No telemetry, analytics, or remote logging SDKs.
- All logging uses `os.Logger` (system console), with cookies and signed URLs marked `privacy: .private`.

## License & disclaimer

MIT-style. Hobby project provided for personal and educational use only. Not affiliated with, endorsed by, or sponsored by YouTube or Google. Downloaded content remains the property of the original rights holders. Users are solely responsible for complying with YouTube's Terms of Service and applicable copyright law.
